# couchbase-capella_app_endpoint_log_streaming_config (Resource)

Manages the Log Streaming config for an App Endpoint on the App Service. This config is used to filter what logs to stream to the log collector that is configured on the App Service.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "couchbase-capella_app_endpoint_logging_config" "new_app_endpoint_logging_config" {
  organization_id   = "aaaaa-bbbb-cccc-dddd-eeee"
  project_id        = "aaaaa-bbbb-cccc-dddd-eeee"
  cluster_id        = "aaaaa-bbbb-cccc-dddd-eeee"
  app_service_id    = "aaaaa-bbbb-cccc-dddd-eeee"
  app_endpoint_name = "<app_endpoint_name>"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `app_endpoint_name` (String) The name of the App Endpoint.
- `app_service_id` (String) The GUID4 ID of the appService.
- `cluster_id` (String) The GUID4 ID of the cluster.
- `log_keys` (Set of String) - Filter logs to specific log keys
- `log_level` (String) - Controls the verbosity of logs based on the specified log level
 - **Valid Values**: `info`, `warn`, `error`
- `organization_id` (String) The GUID4 ID of the organization.
- `project_id` (String) The GUID4 ID of the project.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import couchbase-capella_app_endpoint_log_streaming_config.new_app_endpoint_log_streaming_config app_endpoint_name=<app_endpoint_name>,app_service_id=<app_service_id>,cluster_id=<cluster_id>,project_id=<project_id>,organization_id=<organization_id>
```
